name: Glynne's Lemon Meringue Pie
recipeIngredient: Enough Water to Form a Ball
Juice and Grated Rind of Two Lemons
recipeInstructions:
1. To make the shortcrust pastry, rub the margarine into the flour until it resembles breadcrumbs. Add the orange rind, caster sugar, and mix well. Add the beaten egg and mix well. Gradually add enough water (a tablespoon at a time) to form a ball. Cover with cling film and refrigerate for at least 1/2 hour.
2. To make the filling, combine the juice and grated rind of the lemons in a measuring jug and make up to just less than half a pint with some water. In a separate cup, mix the cornflour with a couple of tablespoons of the lemon mixture until it is smooth. Pour this cornflour mixture and the rest of the lemon and water into a saucepan. Heat over medium heat, stirring continuously until the mixture thickens and the cornflour is cooked out. Allow to cool, stirring occasionally.
3. Remove the pastry from the fridge and roll out, then line an 8-inch flan case/pie dish with it. Prick the base with a fork. Bake blind (lined with foil and baking beans) for 15 minutes in a hot oven (180-200°C) until golden.
4. Remove the baked pastry from the oven and let it cool slightly. Separate the egg yolks from the whites, retaining the yolks. Beat the yolks into the lemon mixture until smooth. Add the sugar to the egg whites and beat until stiff peaks form. Fold in the remaining caster sugar and gently pile on top of the pie.
5. Bake the meringue topping in the oven for a few minutes until nicely browned. Serve hot or cold.
